description: Master the architecture of autonomous systems. From simple ReAct loops to complex multi-agent state machines.

parts.title: Agent Foundations
parts.explanation: Understanding the core loop and reasoning patterns of AI agents.

topics.title: What is an AI Agent?
topics.explanation: An AI Agent is a system that uses a Large Language Model (LLM) as its central reasoning engine to autonomously achieve goals by interacting with environment through tools and loops.

subtopics.title: The Agent Loop
subtopics.explanation: Agents operate in a continuous cycle: Perceive -> Reason -> Act -> Observe. Unlike standard chatbots, agents can decide when to stop or when to retry a failed tool call.

subtopics.title: ReAct Pattern
subtopics.explanation: Reasoning + Acting. The agent verbalizes its 'Thought' before performing an 'Action', then processes the 'Observation'. This significantly improves performance on complex reasoning tasks.

interview_qa.question: What is the difference between a Chain and an Agent?
interview_qa.answer: A Chain is a hardcoded sequence of steps (DAG), whereas an Agent uses the LLM to dynamically decide the next step based on the current state.

topics.title: Tool Definition & Selection
topics.description: Teaching models how to use external APIs.
topics.explanation: Tool use allows agents to extend their capabilities beyond their training data by calling external functions, searching the web, or executing code.

subtopics.title: Semantic Tool Selection
subtopics.explanation: The model uses the tool's description to understand when it should be called. Clear, descriptive names and docstrings are critical for agent reliability.

parts.title: Advanced Architectures
parts.explanation: Scaling agentic systems with state management and collaboration.

topics.title: LangGraph & State Machines
topics.explanation: LangGraph allows developers to build agents as state machines, providing fine-grained control over loops, persistence, and human-in-the-loop interactions.

subtopics.title: Multi-Agent Collaboration
subtopics.explanation: Breaking down large tasks into smaller sub-tasks handled by specialized agents (e.g., a 'Researcher' agent and a 'Writer' agent).
